text: After graduating from high school, Maria chose to go to college, while Omar chose to work full- time. Which of the following best describes the opportunity costs for these decisions?

options.A: Maria’s opportunity cost includes the salary she could have earned if she had gone to work.
options.B: Maria’s opportunity cost is her living expenses while attending college.
options.C: Omar’s opportunity cost is the salary he will earn from working.
options.D: Omar’s opportunity cost is the tuition and expenses he would have paid for college.
options.E: Omar’s opportunity cost is definitely greater than Maria’s.

text: The basic economic problem of all economies is essentially one of deciding how to make the best use of

options.A: unlimited resources to satisfy unlimited wants
options.B: limited resources to satisfy unlimited wants
options.C: unlimited resources to satisfy limited wants
options.D: limited resources to satisfy limited wants
options.E: limited resources to provide public goods The figure shows a graph where the horizontal axis is labeled Quantity and the vertical axis is labeled Price. text: If the government imposes a tariff on imports of cheese, the price and quantity of imported cheese will most likely change in which of the following ways? Price Quantity

options.A: Decrease Decrease
options.B: Decrease Increase
options.C: Increase Not change
options.D: Increase Decrease
options.E: Increase Increase

options.A: a rightward shift in the demand curve for grape juice
options.B: a leftward shift in the demand curve for grape juice
options.C: a decrease in the demand for orange juice, a substitute
options.D: a decrease in the quantity of grape juice demanded

text: The table above shows the demand schedules for Jill and for all other consumers, as well as the market supply schedule for the same good. The equilibrium price in this market is
Which of the following is true of the marginal cost curve?

options.A: It intersects the average variable cost curve and the average total cost curve at each curve’s minimum point.
options.B: It intersects the average variable cost curve and the average fixed cost curve at each curve’s minimum point.
options.C: It lies between the total cost curve and the total variable cost curve.
options.D: It increases initially, for a time, but begins to decline when the point of diminishing returns is reached.
options.E: It decreases, because average variable cost is less than marginal cost.

text: If the marginal cost of producing the first unit of some good is $20 and the marginal cost of producing the second unit is $30, the average variable cost of producing 2 units is
The use of game theory to explain strategic behavior among firms is most associated with which of the following market structures?

options.A: Perfect competition
options.B: Monopolistic competition
options.C: Oligopoly
options.D: Monopoly
options.E: Monopsony

text: All of the following are essential characteristics of a perfectly competitive industry EXCEPT:

options.A: All products produced by the firms in the industry are homogeneous.
options.B: All firms in the industry are price takers.
options.C: All consumers and firms are completely aware of the prices at which transactions take place in the industry.
options.D: There are barriers to entry into and exit from the industry.
options.E: Price is equal to marginal revenue for every firm in the industry. any part of this page is illegal.

text: The marginal revenue product of labor is the

options.A: product price times the wage rate
options.B: additional revenue a firm earns when it employs an additional unit of labor
options.C: increase in the average product of labor when the firm employs an additional unit of labor
options.D: increase in the price of labor when the firm employs an additional unit of labor
options.E: marginal revenue plus product price

text: For a competitive labor market, an increase in which of the following will lead to an increase in the demand for labor?

options.A: The supply of labor
options.B: The minimum wage
options.C: The cost of medical benefits pro- vided to the labor hired by firms
options.D: Labor union dues
options.E: The demand for the good that labor produces

text: Which of the following indicates that a perfectly competitive firm is in long-run equilibrium?

options.A: Price equals marginal cost.
options.B: Price equals average revenue.
options.C: Price equals marginal cost, which equals average total cost.
options.D: Price equals average revenue, which equals marginal revenue.
options.E: Price equals average fixed cost.

text: Which of the following best represents a positive externality?

options.A: Purchasing a pass for an amusement park
options.B: Dining at a favorite restaurant
options.C: Being disturbed by neighboring construction noise
options.D: Enjoying watching birds at a neighbor’s bird feeder
options.E: Dumping waste on someone else’s property

text: Which of the following is a characteristic of a public good?

options.A: It is excludable.
options.B: It is nonrival in consumption.
options.C: It yields substantial spillover costs.
options.D: The private sector would produce a larger than socially optimal quantity.
options.E: Private-sector producers would realize too great a profit.

text: If a perfectly competitive firm is producing where marginal cost is rising and greater than marginal revenue, to maximize profits it should

options.A: increase the level of production
options.B: decrease the level of production
options.C: maintain current level of production
options.D: increase price
options.E: decrease price

text: If cotton is used to produce towels, an increase in the price of cotton will result in which of the following changes in the towel market?

options.A: A decrease in the demand for towels, which leads to a shortage of towels followed by upward pressure on the price of towels
options.B: A decrease in the demand for towels, which leads to a surplus of towels followed by downward pressure on the price of towels
options.C: A decrease in the supply of towels, which leads to a shortage of towels followed by upward pressure on the price of towels
options.D: An increase in the supply of towels, which leads to a surplus of towels followed by downward pressure on the price of towels
options.E: An increase in both the demand and supply of towels, which leads to a surplus of towels followed by upward pressure on the price of towels any part of this page is illegal.

text: Assume that an economy produces televisions and shoes. Which of the following would cause the production possibilities curve for this economy to shift outward?

options.A: An increase in the labor force
options.B: An increase in the prices of both goods
options.C: An increase in the prices of resources used to produce both goods
options.D: A decrease in the demand for shoes
options.E: A change in consumers’ tastes in favor of televisions Goods Price Quantity Purchased (units) Marginal Utility (utils) Soda $1 10 10 Apples $2 5 25

text: Jane spends all her weekly allowance to buy only two goods: soda and apples. According to the table above, if her preferences are characterized by the law of diminishing marginal utility, then which of the following statements is correct?

options.A: Jane is maximizing her utility.
options.B: Jane can buy more apples and less soda to maximize her utility.
options.C: Jane can buy more soda and fewer apples to maximize her utility.
options.D: Jane can buy more apples and the same amount of soda to maximize her utility.
options.E: Jane can buy more soda and the same amount of apples to maximize her utility.

text: If a firm is experiencing economies of scale, which of the following will decrease as output increases?

options.A: Fixed cost
options.B: Long-run total cost
options.C: Long-run average total cost
options.D: Marginal cost
options.E: Marginal revenue

text: Which of the following segments of the marginal cost curve lies entirely on the firm’s short-run supply curve?

options.A: TUV
options.B: STU
options.C: RSTU
options.D: RSTUV
options.E: RS any part of this page is illegal.

text: Assuming a downward-sloping demand curve and an upward-sloping supply curve, which of the following will occur as a result of an increase in the sales tax on a good?

options.A: Supply will increase.
options.B: Demand will increase.
options.C: Quantity supplied will increase.
options.D: Quantity demanded will decrease.
options.E: The price paid by consumers will decrease.

text: Assume that, for a perfectly competitive firm, marginal cost equals average variable cost at $10, marginal cost equals average total cost at $15, and marginal revenue equals marginal cost at $12. On the basis of this information, the firm should

options.A: close down in the short run
options.B: operate in the short run, even though it will sustain a loss
options.C: operate in the short run, because it will make an economic profit of $3 per unit
options.D: operate in the long run, because it will make an economic profit of $3 per unit
options.E: operate in the short run, but decrease output to decrease its cost

text: Which of the following is true for a monopolistically competitive firm in long-run equilibrium?

options.A: It earns a normal profit.
options.B: It sets price equal to marginal cost.
options.C: It is allocatively efficient.
options.D: It is a price taker.
options.E: It produces at minimum average total cost.

text: A monopolist introduces a technological innovation that lowers the marginal cost and average cost of production. The price of the good and the level of output are most likely to change in which of the following ways? Price Level of Output

options.A: Remain constant Remain constant
options.B: Remain constant Increase
options.C: Increase Decrease
options.D: Decrease Increase
options.E: Decrease Remain constant

text: Marginal resource (factor) cost can be defined as

options.A: the change in total resource cost caused by the production of one more unit of output
options.B: the change in total revenue caused by the addition of one more unit of a resource
options.C: the change in total resource cost caused by the addition of one more unit of a resource
options.D: the total resource cost divided by the number of inputs used
options.E: total resource cost divided by output

text: If the government imposes a per-unit tax on the output of a monopoly with a downward-sloping demand curve, the burden of the tax will be

options.A: borne entirely by consumers
options.B: borne entirely by the monopolist
options.C: borne entirely by the government
options.D: shared by the monopolist and the government
options.E: shared by consumers and the monopolist

text: Which of the following is true when there are negative externalities associated with the production of a good?

options.A: The market will adjust automatically to equate marginal social costs and marginal social benefits.
options.B: Marginal social costs will exceed marginal private costs unless businesses are forced to internalize the external costs.
options.C: Marginal private costs will exceed marginal social costs, but the government can correct the problem.
options.D: Producers should be subsidized so that they will produce more of the good.
options.E: Consumers should be subsidized so that they will consume more of the good. any part of this page is illegal. Brazil Spain Corn 2 hours of labor 4 hours of labor Shoes 8 hours of labor 12 hours of labor

text: The table above shows the amount of labor required to produce a unit of corn and a unit of shoes in Brazil and Spain. If both countries have equal numbers of workers, what pattern of international trade between Brazil and Spain is most likely to emerge?

options.A: Brazil will export both corn and shoes.
options.B: Spain will export both corn and shoes.
options.C: No mutually beneficial trade can occur.
options.D: Brazil will export corn and import shoes.
options.E: Brazil will import corn and export shoes.

options.A: Increase Decrease
options.B: Increase No change
options.C: Decrease Increase
options.D: Decrease Decrease
options.E: No change Increase

options.C: First inelastic, then unit elastic throughout
options.D: First elastic, then unit elastic, and finally inelastic
options.E: First inelastic, then unit elastic, and finally elastic

text: One difference between a firm in a perfectly competitive market and an unregulated monopoly is that the

options.A: perfectly competitive firm can increase the quantity it sells at the market price, whereas the monopoly must lower its price to sell more
options.B: perfectly competitive firm sells differentiated products, whereas the monopoly sells a homogeneous product
options.C: price elasticity of demand for a monopoly is much higher than that for a perfectly competitive firm
options.D: monopolist seeks to maximize profits, whereas the perfectly competitive firm maximizes revenues
options.E: monopolist can never change a price, whereas the perfect competitor can

text: Assume that olive oil is produced in a constant- cost, perfectly competitive industry, which is currently in long-run equilibrium. If the current price of olive oil is $5 per quart and the demand for olive oil increases, then the price of olive oil will change in which of the following ways in the short run and long run? Short Run Long Run

options.A: Be more than $5 Be more than $5
options.B: Be more than $5 Be equal to $5
options.C: Be equal to $5 Be equal to $5
options.D: Be less than $5 Be less than $5
options.E: Be less than $5 Be equal to $5

text: Which of the following is always true of a monopoly that is producing a level of output such that marginal revenue is negative?

options.A: It is experiencing economies of scale in production.
options.B: It is maximizing total revenue.
options.C: It is producing where demand is price elastic.
options.D: It could lower price to increase profits.
options.E: It could decrease output to increase profits.

text: A cartel is difficult to maintain for which of the following reasons?

options.A: Consumers substitute away from the good when the price increases.
options.B: Individual cartel members are tempted to cheat on the agreement.
options.C: Although the total gain to cartel members is positive, all members lose when everyone sticks to the agreement.
options.D: Some firms will reduce output in an effort to lower costs of production.
options.E: Oligopolistic behavior is generally predictable.

text: Economists argue that most professional athletes earn economic rent because they

options.A: make additional income through commercial endorsements of products
options.B: are able to participate in sports for only a limited number of years before changing occupations
options.C: earn far more as professional athletes than they could earn in their next-best occupation
options.D: participate in sporting events only about six months during a year
options.E: work less than 40 hours a week during the sport season any part of this page is illegal.

text: A monopolist is inefficient from society’s point of view because

options.A: it overproduces output and charges a price above marginal cost
options.B: it underproduces output and charges a price above marginal cost
options.C: it sometimes colludes with other firms to obtain higher prices
options.D: the price the monopolist charges is too low and it does not advertise
options.E: consumers must pay whatever price the monopolist charges

text: Suppose that at the current output level the mar- ginal social cost of producing a good is greater than its marginal private cost, and that the mar- ginal social benefit associated with production of the good is equal to its marginal private benefit. Which of the following government actions would most likely increase efficiency?

options.A: Taxing the production of the good
options.B: Taxing the production of substitute goods
options.C: Giving tax rebates to people who purchase the good
options.D: Subsidizing production of complementary goods
options.E: Subsidizing consumption of the good

text: Suppose that in one week Sam can knit 5 sweaters or make 4 blankets and Rob can knit 10 sweaters or make 6 blankets. Which of the following is true?

options.A: Sam has an absolute advantage in making blankets.
options.B: Sam has an absolute advantage in knitting sweaters.
options.C: Sam has a comparative advantage in making blankets.
options.D: Sam has a comparative advantage in knitting sweaters.
options.E: Sam has neither a comparative nor an absolute advantage in knitting sweaters or making blankets. any part of this page is illegal.

options.A: Quantity demanded will exceed quantity supplied.
options.B: Quantity supplied will exceed quantity demanded.
options.C: Market price will increase.
options.D: Market price will be unaffected.
options.E: Market price will decrease.

text: An increase in the supply of coffee could be caused by

options.A: a decrease in the price of cream, which is a complement to coffee
options.B: a decrease in the cost of labor used to produce coffee
options.C: an increase in consumer income
options.D: an increase in the demand for coffee
options.E: an increase in the price of coffee

text: Which of the following explains why a decrease in the price of a normal good will lead to an increase in the quantity demanded of the good?

options.A: A lower price will increase consumers’ purchasing power.
options.B: A lower price will increase consumers’ marginal utility.
options.C: A lower price will increase demand for the good.
options.D: A lower price will increase demand for substitute goods.
options.E: A lower price will decrease demand for complementary goods.

options.C: marginal revenue is positive throughout the range of the demand curve
options.D: the diamond industry consists of a few firms selling similar diamonds

text: Over the past 5 years, 50 new restaurants have opened and 30 have closed in the city of Zuni. Currently there are 110 restaurants operating in the city. options.D: Monopolistic competition High Positive

options.C: A decrease in the price of an input and a decrease in total industry profits
options.D: A decrease in total industry sales

text: If a good is available free of charge, an individual will consume it until

options.A: marginal utility is zero
options.B: average utility is zero
options.C: total utility is zero
options.D: marginal utility equals average utility
options.E: marginal utility equals total utility

text: If a competitive firm pays its workers the value of the marginal product of the last worker hired, which of the following is true?

options.A: The firm will not earn any economic profits.
options.B: Workers will look for employment elsewhere.
options.C: The wage will be less than the marginal product.
options.D: The firm will not maximize profits.
options.E: The contribution of the last worker hired to the firm’s profit will be zero.

text: Which of the following is necessary for a firm to practice price discrimination?

options.A: The government strictly enforces antitrust laws.
options.B: The firm is a member of a cartel.
options.C: The firm can prevent resale of its goods.
options.D: The demand curve for the product is perfectly inelastic.
options.E: The demand curve for the product is perfectly elastic.

text: Country A has a Gini coefficient of 0.5, and Country B has a Gini coefficient of 0.3. Which of the following statements is definitely true?

options.B: Country B has higher average income than Country A.
options.C: Income is more evenly distributed in Country A than in Country B.
options.D: Income is more evenly distributed in Country B than in Country A.
options.E: Country A has more deadweight loss than Country B.

options.B: less than the profit-maximizing level of output
options.C: at a level of output where average total cost is at a minimum
options.D: at a level of output where marginal costs are rising
options.E: at a level of output where average variable costs are falling any part of this page is illegal.The figure shows a graph whose horizontal axis is labeled Number of Workers and the vertical axis is labeled Wage Rate in dollars.
